title: Perihelion v1.4.0
content:
Perihelion moved from recovery mode back into a reliable working archive. This pass focused on restoring the core library workflow, aligning the backend with the current gallery UI, and making saved share work reusable instead of disposable.

What changed:

  • Library Root Alignment: Updated the image-service root logic to prefer E:\images, fall back to the legacy E:\perihelion\images path, and allow an explicit PERIHELION_IMAGE_ROOT override. This resolved the gallery-wide Folder not found failure after the image library move.
  • Tag / List / Detail Backend Pass: Added backend support for persistent image details, tag listing, bulk tag operations, tag rename/delete, share-list enumeration, share editing, and share deletion so the Perihelion frontend and Python API are back in sync.
  • Share Reuse Workflow: Share codes still open their dedicated public page, but now they can also be loaded directly back into the live gallery selection to continue work. Missing files are preserved as placeholders so old shares remain intelligible even when part of the library has changed.
  • Gallery Pagination Fix: Corrected the gallery footer and page controls so they respect backend total and totalPages values instead of treating the first fetched page as the whole archive.
  • Central Auth Download Stability: Fixed the top-right single-file download path so central-auth users no longer trigger a local SQLite foreign-key error when Perihelion records download history.

This checkpoint returns Perihelion to a strong day-to-day working state while keeping room for later visual polish.
